source_pr.title: dxf: make api request metering more accurate by intercepting request call
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #61702

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?
previously, we basically based on the formula `total file size / part-size + 2` to estimate the PUT API call count, and use reader reload as the timing to get the GET request call count, so it's not accurate, and make the UT hard to check the count.
and previous way pollute normal code too much, we have to pass many params into the `external`/`local` pkg to instruct where we read/write to get the request count.
and some PUT/GET is not collected, such as subtask meta file read/write.
so we change to counting the API call directly by intercepting those request call. and revert the old logic introduced in https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/63431.
- for S3/KS3, we use it's `Send` handlers
- for GCS, we use a wrapped round tripper.


we also avoid create object storage for each call of decodeBackfillSubTaskMeta and writeExternalBackfillSubTaskMeta in add-index. and add integration test for metering

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

import-into S3, as we will use HEAD to check whether the subtask meta file exists, so we have 2 more GETs, one for each of encode and merge-sort step, those 2 steps will update the subtask meta after the subtask finishes
```
[2025/11/11 13:54:00.009 +08:00] [INFO] [metering.go:176] ["recorder unregistered and finished final flush"] [keyspaceName=SYSTEM] [component=meter] [accumulatedData="{taskID: 5, keyspace: SYSTEM, type: ImportInto, getReqs: 8, putReqs: 6, readBytes: 2, writeBytes: 29}"]
```

import-into on KS3
```
[2025/11/11 14:02:00.015 +08:00] [INFO] [metering.go:176] ["recorder unregistered and finished final flush"] [keyspaceName=SYSTEM] [component=meter] [accumulatedData="{taskID: 30001, keyspace: SYSTEM, type: ImportInto, getReqs: 6, putReqs: 6, readBytes: 2, writeBytes: 29}"]
```

add-index on S3
```
[2025/11/11 20:37:00.022 +08:00] [INFO] [metering.go:185] ["recorder unregistered and finished final flush"] [keyspaceName=SYSTEM] [component=meter] [accumulatedData="{id: 1, keyspace: SYSTEM, type: backfill, requests{get: 4, put: 3}, read: 132B, write: 114B}"]
```

- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

fix_pattern: previously, we basically based on the formula `total file size / part-size + 2` to estimate the PUT API call count, and use reader reload as the timing to get the GET request call count, so it's not accurate, and make the UT hard to check the count. and previous way pollute normal code too much, we have to pass many params into the `external`/`local` pkg to instruct where we read/write to get the request count. and some PUT/GET is not collected, such as subtask meta file read/write. so we change to counting the API call directly by intercepting those request call. and revert the old logic introduced in https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/63431. for S3/KS3, we use it's `Send` handlers for GCS, we use a wrapped round tripper.
